title: Reducing T-count with the ZX-calculus
abstract: Represents a Clifford+T circuit as a ZX-diagram, simplifies it with the graph-theoretic ZX rewrite rules, and introduces 'phase teleportation' to let non-Clifford phases combine and cancel non-locally without changing circuit structure. Matches or beats prior T-count reducers on most benchmarks, with up to ~50% reduction in the ancilla-free case.
relevance_note: Phase teleportation / phase-gadget fusion: merges and cancels T-phases scattered across the circuit, cutting T-count up to ~50% with no extra qubits. Core technique behind PyZX. Directly applicable to reducing T in the modular-arithmetic blocks of ECC point addition.

title: Graph-theoretic Simplification of Quantum Circuits with the ZX-calculus
abstract: Develops the local-complementation and pivoting rewrite strategy that removes interior Clifford spiders from a ZX graph (graph-like normal form), giving a deterministic simplification routine that reduces the number of nodes (and 2-qubit gates) while preserving semantics.
relevance_note: Local-complementation + pivoting to eliminate interior Clifford spiders: the foundational ZX simplification that downstream T-count reducers build on. Reduces spider/edge count and CNOT count; the substrate for phase-teleportation T-count cuts.

title: Polynomial-Time T-Depth Optimization of Clifford+T Circuits Via Matroid Partitioning (T-par)
abstract: Models the Clifford+T circuit as a phase polynomial over parity terms and uses matroid partitioning to schedule T/T-dagger rotations in parallel layers, minimizing T-depth (and exposing T-count cancellations) in polynomial time. Basis of the 'T-par' optimizer.
relevance_note: Phase-polynomial + matroid partitioning ('T-par'): groups Rz(pi/4) rotations on identical parities so they cancel/merge, reducing both T-count and T-depth. Strong on arithmetic circuits like adders used in ECC.

title: T-count optimization and Reed-Muller codes
abstract: Shows the T-count minimization of a phase polynomial is equivalent to minimum-distance decoding of a punctured Reed-Muller code, giving optimal and heuristic (RM-based) algorithms that provably reduce T-count below earlier methods on many circuits.
relevance_note: Reed-Muller / coding-theoretic T-count minimization: reformulates T-count as RM(1,m) decoding, enabling provably better T-count for the phase-polynomial blocks (e.g. multiple-control gates, modular arithmetic).

title: Novel constructions for the fault-tolerant Toffoli gate
abstract: Gives a Toffoli realized with Clifford operations plus only four T gates (vs the conventional seven) using measurement and classical feedforward; adding n controls costs 4n T gates instead of 8n. Also provides an error-detecting variant.
relevance_note: Measurement-assisted Toffoli with 4 T instead of 7 (and 4n vs 8n for multi-controlled). Roughly halves the T-cost of every Toffoli; central lever for ECC point-addition circuits dominated by Toffoli/AND structure.

title: Halving the cost of quantum addition (measurement-based / temporary AND gates)
abstract: Introduces the measurement-uncomputed 'temporary logical-AND' gadget: an AND can be computed with 4 T gates and uncomputed with 0 T gates (Clifford + measurement + feedforward), and ripple-carry addition reuses a single ancilla, halving Toffoli/T cost relative to standard adders.
relevance_note: Gidney AND-gate: compute-AND = 4 T, uncompute-AND = 0 T via measurement. Halves the T-count of adders/comparators. The standard building block for cheap reversible modular arithmetic in ECC circuits.

title: On the advantages of using relative phase Toffolis with an application to multiple control Toffoli optimization
abstract: Shows that relative-phase (up-to-phase) Toffoli gates cost as few as 4 T gates and that multiple-control Toffoli gates can be built from relative-phase pieces whose spurious phases cancel between the compute and uncompute halves, cutting T-count and ancilla.
relevance_note: Relative-phase Toffoli: 4 T instead of 7 when a spurious controlled phase is later uncomputed. Composing MCT gates from relative-phase Toffolis (compute/uncompute cancel) sharply lowers T-count and removes ancilla.

title: Elementary gates for quantum computation
abstract: Foundational gate-count paper: upper/lower bounds for building 2- and 3-bit gates, asymptotic counts for n-bit Deutsch-Toffoli gates, and the canonical recursive decomposition of multi-controlled-U / multi-controlled-Toffoli into elementary gates (with and without ancilla).
relevance_note: Original multi-controlled-Toffoli decomposition (linear-with-ancilla and quadratic-without). Baseline against which all later MCT/T reductions (Maslov, Jones, He et al.) are measured; defines the ancilla/gate trade-offs.

title: Techniques for the Synthesis of Reversible Toffoli Networks (template/peephole matching)
abstract: Presents template (peephole) simplification of reversible Toffoli networks: a library of size-classified templates whose matched subsequences are replaced by shorter equivalents, plus a fast template-application algorithm and new templates of sizes 7 and 9.
relevance_note: Template / peephole matching: replaces locally matching gate runs with shorter equivalents and removes adjacent cancelling gates, reducing Toffoli/gate count in reversible logic. The classical complement to ZX-level optimization.

title: Quantum Circuit Optimization with AlphaTensor (AlphaTensor-Quantum)
abstract: Casts T-count minimization as symmetric tensor (rank) decomposition and solves it with a deep-RL agent (extension of AlphaTensor), incorporating gadget-based domain knowledge. Outperforms prior T-count optimizers on arithmetic benchmarks and rediscovers Karatsuba-like finite-field multiplication.
relevance_note: RL + tensor-rank decomposition for T-count: finds gadgetized low-T circuits, beating heuristic optimizers on Shor-relevant arithmetic (finite-field multiplication). Directly targets the kind of modular-multiplication blocks in ECC point addition.

title: Reusability Report: Optimizing T-count in General Quantum Circuits with AlphaTensor-Quantum
abstract: Independent reusability evaluation of AlphaTensor-Quantum: reproduces and probes the deep-RL T-count optimizer on general (non-arithmetic) circuits, characterizing where the gadget-based tensor-decomposition approach helps and where it underperforms classical ZX/phase-polynomial methods.
relevance_note: Critical follow-up on AlphaTensor-Quantum: clarifies the practical T-count gains/limits of the RL approach vs ZX and T-par on general circuits, useful for deciding whether RL is worth deploying on ECC arithmetic.

title: Quartz: Superoptimization of Quantum Circuits
abstract: Automatically generates candidate circuit-rewrite rules for an arbitrary gate set, verifies them with an automated theorem prover, then applies them via cost-based backtracking search. Discovers optimizations beyond hand-written rule sets across multiple gate sets.
relevance_note: Automated rewrite-rule generation + verified superoptimization: discovers gate-count/T-count-reducing transformations a human might miss, generalizing template matching to any gate set. Tooling for finding new ECC-circuit simplifications.

title: A Verified Optimizer for Quantum Circuits (VOQC)
abstract: A formally verified (Coq) quantum circuit optimizer implementing gate cancellation, rotation merging, and not-propagation passes proven to preserve semantics; competitive with Qiskit/t|ket> while guaranteeing correctness.
relevance_note: Formally verified rotation-merging and gate-cancellation passes: machine-checked T/gate reduction, so optimizations applied to ECC arithmetic are provably correct (no silent miscompilation of the circuit).

title: Reinforcement Learning Based Quantum Circuit Optimization via ZX-Calculus
abstract: Trains an RL agent to choose and order ZX-calculus rewrite rules (local complementation, pivoting, phase gadgetization) to minimize gate/T-count, outperforming fixed-heuristic ZX simplification routines such as PyZX's full_reduce on benchmark circuits.
relevance_note: RL-guided ZX rewriting: learns the rewrite-ordering policy that fixed PyZX heuristics miss, squeezing extra 2-qubit-gate and T-count reduction out of the ZX representation. Applicable on top of phase teleportation.

title: Adaptive Clifford+T Decomposition of Large Toffoli Gates with One Clean Ancilla
abstract: Gives measurement-adaptive Clifford+T constructions for multi-controlled Toffoli (MCX) gates using only one clean ancilla, lowering T-count and T-depth versus standard ancilla-heavy decompositions by exploiting mid-circuit measurement and feedforward.
relevance_note: Measurement-adaptive multi-controlled Toffoli with a single clean ancilla: reduces both T-count and ancilla for large MCX gates - relevant when ECC circuits need wide controlled operations under tight qubit budgets.
